 47→        buildConfigField "String", "ACRA_URL", '"https://ankidroid.org/acra/report"'
 48→        buildConfigField "String", "BACKEND_VERSION", "\"$ankidroid_backend_version\""
 49→        buildConfigField "Boolean", "ENABLE_LEAK_CANARY", "false"
 50→        buildConfigField "Boolean", "ALLOW_UNSAFE_MIGRATION", "false"
 51→        buildConfigField "String", "GIT_COMMIT_HASH", "\"${gitCommitHash()}\""
 52→        buildConfigField "long", "BUILD_TIME", System.currentTimeMillis().toString()
 53→        resValue "string", "app_name", "AnkiDroid"
 54→
 55→        // The version number is of the form:
 56→        // <major>.<minor>.<maintenance>[dev|alpha<build>|beta<build>|]
 57→        // The <build> is only present for alpha and beta releases (e.g., 2.0.4alpha2 or 2.0.4beta4), developer builds do
 58→        // not have a build number (e.g., 2.0.4dev) and official releases only have three components (e.g., 2.0.4).
 59→        //
 60→        // The version code is derived from the version name as follows:
 61→        // AbbCCtDD
 62→        // A: 1-digit decimal number representing the major version
 63→        // bb: 2-digit decimal number representing the minor version
 64→        // CC: 2-digit decimal number representing the maintenance version
 65→        // t: 1-digit decimal number representing the type of the build
 66→        // 0: developer build
 67→        // 1: alpha release
 68→        // 2: beta release
 69→        // 3: public release
 70→        // DD: 2-digit decimal number representing the build
 71→        // 00 for internal builds and public releases
 72→        // alpha/beta build number for alpha/beta releases
 73→        //
 74→        // This ensures the correct ordering between the various types of releases (dev < alpha < beta < release) which is
 75→        // needed for upgrades to be offered correctly.
 76→        versionCode=21800105
 77→        versionName="2.18alpha5"
 78→        minSdk 23 // also in testlib/build.gradle.kts
 79→        // After #13695: change .tests_emulator.yml
 80→        targetSdk 33 // also in [api|testlib]/build.gradle.kts and ../robolectricDownloader.gradle
 81→        testApplicationId "com.ichi2.anki.tests"
 82→        vectorDrawables.useSupportLibrary = true
 83→        testInstrumentationRunner 'com.ichi2.testutils.NewCollectionPathTestRunner'
 84→    }
 85→    signingConfigs {
 86→        release {
 87→            storeFile file("${homePath}/src/android-keystore")
 88→            keyAlias "nrkeystorealias"
 89→            storePassword System.getenv("KSTOREPWD")
 90→            keyPassword System.getenv("KEYPWD")
 91→        }
 92→    }
 93→    buildTypes {
 94→        named('debug') {
 95→            versionNameSuffix "-debug"
 96→            debuggable true
 97→            applicationIdSuffix ".debug"
 98→            splits.abi.universalApk = true // Build universal APK for debug always
 99→            // Check Crash Reports page on developer wiki for info on ACRA testing
100→            // buildConfigField "String", "ACRA_URL", '"https://918f7f55-f238-436c-b34f-c8b5f1331fe5-bluemix.cloudant.com/acra-ankidroid/_design/acra-storage/_update/report"'
101→            if (project.rootProject.file('local.properties').exists()) {
102→                Properties localProperties = new Properties()
103→                localProperties.load(project.rootProject.file('local.properties').newDataInputStream())
104→                // #6009 Allow optional disabling of JaCoCo for general build (assembleDebug).
105→                // jacocoDebug task was slow, hung, and wasn't required unless I wanted coverage
106→                testCoverageEnabled localProperties['enable_coverage'] != "false"
107→                // not profiled: optimization for build times
108→                if (localProperties['enable_languages'] == "false") {
109→                    android.defaultConfig.resConfigs "en"
110→                }
111→                // allows the scoped storage migration when the user is not logged in
112→                if (localProperties["allow_unsafe_migration"] != null) {
113→                    buildConfigField "Boolean", "ALLOW_UNSAFE_MIGRATION", localProperties["allow_unsafe_migration"]
114→                }
115→                // allow disabling leak canary
116→                if (localProperties["enable_leak_canary"] != null) {
117→                    buildConfigField "Boolean", "ENABLE_LEAK_CANARY", localProperties["enable_leak_canary"]
118→                } else {
119→                    buildConfigField "Boolean", "ENABLE_LEAK_CANARY", "true"
120→                }
121→            } else {
122→                testCoverageEnabled true
123→            }
124→
125→            // make the icon red if in debug mode
126→            resValue 'color', 'anki_foreground_icon_color_0', "#FFFF0000"
127→            resValue 'color', 'anki_foreground_icon_color_1', "#FFFF0000"
128→            resValue "string", "applicationId", "${defaultConfig.applicationId}${applicationIdSuffix}"
129→        }
130→        named('release') {
131→            minifyEnabled true
132→            splits.abi.universalApk = universalApkEnabled // Build universal APK for release with `-Duniversal-apk=true`
133→            proguardFiles getDefaultProguardFile('proguard-android.txt'), 'proguard-rules.pro'
134→            signingConfig signingConfigs.release
135→
136→            // syntax: assembleRelease -PcustomSuffix="suffix" -PcustomName="New name"
137→            if (project.hasProperty("customSuffix")) {
138→                // the suffix needs a '.' at the start
139→                applicationIdSuffix project.property("customSuffix").replaceFirst(/^\.*/, ".")
140→                resValue "string", "applicationId", "${defaultConfig.applicationId}${applicationIdSuffix}"
141→            } else {
142→                resValue "string", "applicationId", defaultConfig.applicationId
143→            }
144→            if (project.hasProperty("customName")) {
145→                resValue "string", "app_name", project.property("customName")
146→            }
147→
148→            resValue 'color', 'anki_foreground_icon_color_0', "#FF29B6F6"
149→            resValue 'color', 'anki_foreground_icon_color_1', "#FF0288D1"
150→        }
151→    }
152→
153→    /**
154→     * Product Flavors are used for Amazon App Store and Google Play Store.
155→     * This is because we cannot use Camera Permissions in Amazon App Store (for FireTv etc...)
156→     * Therefore, different AndroidManifest for Camera Permissions is used in Amazon flavor.
157→     *
158→     * This flavor block must stay in sync with the same block in testlib/build.gradle.kts
159→     */
160→    flavorDimensions += "appStore"
161→    productFlavors {
162→        create('play') {
163→            dimension "appStore"
164→        }
165→        create('amazon') {
166→            dimension "appStore"
167→        }
168→        // A 'full' build has no restrictions on storage/camera. Distributed on GitHub/F-Droid
169→        create('full') {
170→            dimension "appStore"
171→        }
172→    }
173→
174→    /**
175→     * Set this to true to create five separate APKs instead of one:
176→     *   - 2 APKs that only work on ARM/ARM64 devices
177→     *   - 2 APKs that only works on x86/x86_64 devices
178→     *   - a universal APK that works on all devices
179→     * The advantage is the size of most APKs is reduced by about 2.5MB.
180→     * Upload all the APKs to the Play Store and people will download
181→     * the correct one based on the CPU architecture of their device.
182→     */
183→    def enableSeparateBuildPerCPUArchitecture = true
184→
185→    splits {
186→        abi {
187→            reset()
188→            enable enableSeparateBuildPerCPUArchitecture
189→            //universalApk enableUniversalApk  // set in debug + release config blocks above
190→            include "armeabi-v7a", "x86", "arm64-v8a", "x86_64"
191→        }
192→    }
193→    // applicationVariants are e.g. debug, release
194→    applicationVariants.configureEach { variant ->
195→        // We want the same version stream for all ABIs in debug but for release we can split them
196→        if (variant.buildType.name == 'release') {
197→            variant.outputs.configureEach { output ->
198→
199→                // For each separate APK per architecture, set a unique version code as described here:
200→                // https://developer.android.com/studio/build/configure-apk-splits.html
201→                def versionCodes = ["armeabi-v7a": 1, "x86": 2, "arm64-v8a": 3, "x86_64": 4]
202→                def outputFile = output.outputFile
203→                if (outputFile != null && outputFile.name.endsWith('.apk')) {
204→                    def abi = output.getFilter("ABI")
205→                    if (abi != null) {  // null for the universal-debug, universal-release variants
206→                        //  From: https://developer.android.com/studio/publish/versioning#appversioning
207→                        //  "Warning: The greatest value Google Play allows for versionCode is 2100000000"
208→                        //  AnkiDroid versionCodes have a budget 8 digits (through AnkiDroid 9)
209→                        //  This style does ABI version code ranges with the 9th digit as 0-4.
210→                        //  This consumes ~20% of the version range space, w/50 years of versioning at our major-version pace
211→                        output.versionCodeOverride =
212→                                // ex:  321200106 = 3 * 100000000 + 21200106
213→                                versionCodes.get(abi) * 100000000 + defaultConfig.versionCode
214→                    }
215→                }
216→            }
217→        }
218→    }
219→
220→    testOptions {
221→        animationsDisabled true
222→        kotlinOptions {
223→            freeCompilerArgs += [
224→                    '-opt-in=kotlinx.coroutines.ExperimentalCoroutinesApi',
225→            ]
226→        }
227→    }

231→        targetCompatibility JavaVersion.VERSION_17

233→    }
234→    kotlinOptions {

236→    }

241→        }
242→    }
243→}

251→// Install Git pre-commit hook for Ktlint
252→tasks.register('installGitHook', Copy) {
253→    from new File(rootProject.rootDir, 'pre-commit')
254→    into { new File(rootProject.rootDir, '.git/hooks') }
255→    fileMode 0755
256→}

259→
260→// Issue 11078 - some emulators run, but run zero tests, and still report success
261→tasks.register('assertNonzeroAndroidTests') {
262→    doLast {
263→        // androidTest currently creates one .xml file per emulator with aggregate results in this dir
264→        File folder = file("./build/outputs/androidTest-results/connected/flavors/play")
265→        File[] listOfFiles = folder.listFiles({ d, f -> f ==~ /.*.xml/ } as FilenameFilter)
266→        for (File file : listOfFiles) {
267→            // The aggregate results file currently contains a line with this pattern holding test count
268→            String[] matches = file.readLines().findAll { it.contains('<testsuite') }
269→            if (matches.length != 1) {
270→                throw new GradleScriptException("Unable to determine count of tests executed for " + file.name + ". Regex pattern out of date?", null)
271→            }
272→            if (!(matches[0] ==~ /.* tests="\d+" .*/) || matches[0].contains('tests="0"')) {
273→                throw new GradleScriptException("androidTest executed 0 tests for " + file.name + " - Probably a bug with the emulator. Try another image.", null)
274→            }
275→        }
276→    }
277→}
278→afterEvaluate {
279→    tasks.named('connectedPlayDebugAndroidTest').configure { finalizedBy('assertNonzeroAndroidTests') }
280→}

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.preferences
 17→
 18→import android.os.Bundle
 19→import android.view.View
 20→import android.view.ViewGroup
 21→import androidx.appcompat.app.AppCompatActivity
 22→import androidx.preference.Preference
 23→import androidx.preference.PreferenceFragmentCompat
 24→import com.bytehamster.lib.preferencesearch.SearchConfiguration
 25→import com.bytehamster.lib.preferencesearch.SearchPreference
 26→import com.ichi2.anki.BuildConfig
 27→import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager
 28→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 29→import com.ichi2.compat.CompatHelper
 30→import com.ichi2.preferences.HeaderPreference
 31→import com.ichi2.utils.AdaptionUtil
 32→
 33→class HeaderFragment : PreferenceFragmentCompat() {
 34→    private var selectedHeaderPreference: HeaderPreference? = null
 35→    private var selectedHeaderPreferenceKey: String = DEFAULT_SELECTED_HEADER
 36→    override fun onCreatePreferences(savedInstanceState: Bundle?, rootKey: String?) {
 37→        setPreferencesFromResource(R.xml.preference_headers, rootKey)
 38→
 39→        selectedHeaderPreferenceKey = savedInstanceState?.getString(KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF) ?: DEFAULT_SELECTED_HEADER
 40→
 41→        highlightHeaderPreference(requirePreference<HeaderPreference>(selectedHeaderPreferenceKey))
 42→
 43→        requirePreference<HeaderPreference>(R.string.pref_backup_limits_screen_key)
 44→            .title = CollectionManager.TR.preferencesBackups()
 45→
 46→        requirePreference<Preference>(R.string.pref_advanced_screen_key).apply {
 47→            if (AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
 48→                isVisible = false
 49→            }
 50→        }
 51→
 52→        if (DevOptionsFragment.isEnabled(requireContext())) {
 53→            setDevOptionsVisibility(true)
 54→        }
 55→
 56→        configureSearchBar(
 57→            requireActivity() as AppCompatActivity,
 58→            requirePreference<SearchPreference>(R.string.search_preference_key).searchConfiguration
 59→        )
 60→    }
 61→
 62→    private fun highlightHeaderPreference(headerPreference: HeaderPreference) {
 63→        if (!(activity as Preferences).hasLateralNavigation()) {
 64→            return
 65→        }
 66→        selectedHeaderPreference?.setHighlighted(false)
 67→        // highlight the newly selected header
 68→        selectedHeaderPreference = headerPreference.apply {
 69→            setHighlighted(true)
 70→            selectedHeaderPreferenceKey = this.key
 71→        }
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    override fun onPreferenceTreeClick(preference: Preference): Boolean {
 75→        highlightHeaderPreference(preference as HeaderPreference)
 76→        return super.onPreferenceTreeClick(preference)
 77→    }
 78→
 79→    override fun onSaveInstanceState(outState: Bundle) {
 80→        super.onSaveInstanceState(outState)
 81→        outState.putString(KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF, selectedHeaderPreferenceKey)
 82→    }
 83→
 84→    override fun onViewStateRestored(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 85→        super.onViewStateRestored(savedInstanceState)
 86→        highlightHeaderPreference(requirePreference<HeaderPreference>(selectedHeaderPreferenceKey))
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    override fun onStart() {
 90→        super.onStart()
 91→        requireActivity().setTitle(R.string.settings)
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    override fun onViewCreated(view: View, savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 95→        super.onViewCreated(view, savedInstanceState)
 96→        // use the same fragment container to search in case there is a navigation container
 97→        requirePreference<SearchPreference>(R.string.search_preference_key)
 98→            .searchConfiguration
 99→            .setFragmentContainerViewId((view.parent as? ViewGroup)?.id ?: R.id.settings_container)
100→    }
101→
102→    fun setDevOptionsVisibility(isVisible: Boolean) {
103→        requirePreference<Preference>(R.string.pref_dev_options_screen_key).isVisible = isVisible
104→    }
105→
106→    companion object {
107→        private const val KEY_SELECTED_HEADER_PREF = "selected_header_pref"
108→        private const val DEFAULT_SELECTED_HEADER = "generalScreen"
109→
110→        fun configureSearchBar(activity: AppCompatActivity, searchConfiguration: SearchConfiguration) {
111→            with(searchConfiguration) {
112→                setActivity(activity)
113→                setBreadcrumbsEnabled(true)
114→                setFuzzySearchEnabled(false)
115→                setHistoryEnabled(true)
116→
117→                index(R.xml.preferences_general)
118→                index(R.xml.preferences_reviewing)
119→                index(R.xml.preferences_sync)
120→                index(R.xml.preferences_custom_sync_server)
121→                    .addBreadcrumb(R.string.pref_cat_sync)
122→                index(R.xml.preferences_notifications)
123→                index(R.xml.preferences_appearance)
124→                index(R.xml.preferences_custom_buttons)
125→                    .addBreadcrumb(R.string.pref_cat_appearance)
126→                index(R.xml.preferences_controls)
127→                index(R.xml.preferences_accessibility)
128→                index(R.xml.preferences_backup_limits)
129→            }
130→
131→            // Some preferences and categories are only shown conditionally,
132→            // so they should be searchable based on the same conditions
133→
134→            /** From [HeaderFragment.onCreatePreferences] */
135→            if (DevOptionsFragment.isEnabled(activity)) {
136→                searchConfiguration.index(R.xml.preferences_dev_options)
137→                /** From [DevOptionsFragment.initSubscreen] */
138→                if (BuildConfig.DEBUG) {
139→                    searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.dev_options_enabled_by_user_key))
140→                }
141→            }
142→
143→            /** From [HeaderFragment.onCreatePreferences] */
144→            if (!AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
145→                searchConfiguration.index(R.xml.preferences_advanced)
146→            }
147→
148→            /** From [NotificationsSettingsFragment.initSubscreen] */
149→            if (AdaptionUtil.isXiaomiRestrictedLearningDevice) {
150→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.pref_notifications_vibrate_key))
151→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.pref_notifications_blink_key))
152→            }
153→
154→            /** From [AdvancedSettingsFragment.removeUnnecessaryAdvancedPrefs] */
155→            if (!CompatHelper.hasScrollKeys()) {
156→                searchConfiguration.ignorePreference(activity.getString(R.string.double_scrolling_gap_key))

 30→import com.ichi2.anki.launchCatchingTask

 38→import com.ichi2.preferences.IncrementerNumberRangePreferenceCompat
 39→import com.ichi2.preferences.NumberRangePreferenceCompat.ShouldShowDialog
 40→import kotlinx.coroutines.CancellationException
 41→import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.MutableStateFlow
 42→import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
 43→
 44→sealed interface State {
 45→    data object Fetching : State
 46→    class Fetched(val backupLimits: BackupLimits) : State
 47→    sealed interface Error : State {
 48→        data object NoCollection : Error
 49→        class Exception(val exception: kotlin.Exception) : Error
 50→    }
 51→}
 52→
 53→class BackupLimitsViewModel : ViewModel(), CollectionDirectoryProvider {
 54→    override val collectionDirectory = CollectionManager.getCollectionDirectory()
 55→
 56→    val flowOfState = MutableStateFlow<State>(State.Fetching)
 57→
 58→    fun launchFetchingOfBackupLimits() = viewModelScope.launch {
 59→        flowOfState.emit(
 60→            try {
 61→                ensureCanWriteToOrCreateCollectionDirectory()
 62→                if (!collectionDirectoryExists()) {
 63→                    State.Error.NoCollection
 64→                } else {
 65→                    val backupLimits = withCol { backend.getPreferences().backups }
 66→                    State.Fetched(backupLimits)
 67→                }
 68→            } catch (e: CancellationException) {
 69→                throw e
 70→            } catch (e: Exception) {
 71→                State.Error.Exception(e)
 72→            }
 73→        )
 74→    }
 75→
 76→    suspend fun updateBackupLimits(block: BackupLimits.Builder.() -> Unit) {
 77→        withCol {
 78→            val preferences = backend.getPreferences()
 79→            val backups = preferences.backups.toBuilder().apply(block).build()
 80→            backend.setPreferences(preferences.toBuilder().setBackups(backups).build())
 81→        }
 82→
 83→        launchFetchingOfBackupLimits()
 84→    }
 85→}
 86→
 87→/**
 88→ * A backup limit preferences presenter for [PreferenceFragmentCompat].
 89→ * Adds preferences from [R.xml.preferences_backup_limits] to current hierarchy, and manages them.
 90→ *
 91→ * To use, simply say
 92→ *
 93→ *     val backupLimitsPresenter = BackupLimitsPresenter()
 94→ *     backupLimitsPresenter.observeLifecycle()
 95→ *
 96→ * To refresh in case of changes on disk or in schema, say
 97→ *
 98→ *     backupLimitsPresenter.refresh()
 99→ */
100→class BackupLimitsPresenter(private val fragment: PreferenceFragmentCompat) : DefaultLifecycleObserver {
101→    private val viewModel: BackupLimitsViewModel by fragment.viewModels()
102→
103→    private lateinit var backupsHelpPreference: HtmlHelpPreference
104→    private lateinit var minutesBetweenAutomaticBackupsPreference: IncrementerNumberRangePreferenceCompat
105→    private lateinit var dailyBackupsToKeepPreference: IncrementerNumberRangePreferenceCompat
106→    private lateinit var weeklyBackupsToKeepPreference: IncrementerNumberRangePreferenceCompat
107→    private lateinit var monthlyBackupsToKeepPreference: IncrementerNumberRangePreferenceCompat
108→
109→    override fun onCreate(owner: LifecycleOwner) {
110→        backupsHelpPreference = fragment.requirePreference(R.string.pref_backups_help_key)
111→        minutesBetweenAutomaticBackupsPreference = fragment.requirePreference(R.string.pref_minutes_between_automatic_backups_key)
112→        dailyBackupsToKeepPreference = fragment.requirePreference(R.string.pref_daily_backups_to_keep_key)
113→        weeklyBackupsToKeepPreference = fragment.requirePreference(R.string.pref_weekly_backups_to_keep_key)
114→        monthlyBackupsToKeepPreference = fragment.requirePreference(R.string.pref_monthly_backups_to_keep_key)
115→
116→        minutesBetweenAutomaticBackupsPreference
117→            .launchWhenChanged<Int> { viewModel.updateBackupLimits { minimumIntervalMins = it } }
118→        dailyBackupsToKeepPreference
119→            .launchWhenChanged<Int> { viewModel.updateBackupLimits { daily = it } }
120→        weeklyBackupsToKeepPreference
121→            .launchWhenChanged<Int> { viewModel.updateBackupLimits { weekly = it } }
122→        monthlyBackupsToKeepPreference
123→            .launchWhenChanged<Int> { viewModel.updateBackupLimits { monthly = it } }
124→
125→        // TODO Make NumberRangePreferenceCompat, and perhaps other preferences,
126→        //   aware of the idea that the value may not have loaded yet, and simplify this.
127→        listOf(
128→            minutesBetweenAutomaticBackupsPreference,
129→            dailyBackupsToKeepPreference,
130→            weeklyBackupsToKeepPreference,
131→            monthlyBackupsToKeepPreference
132→        ).forEach { preference ->
133→            preference.summaryProvider = Preference.SummaryProvider<EditTextPreference> {
134→                when (viewModel.flowOfState.value) {
135→                    is State.Fetching -> fragment.getString(R.string.pref__etc__summary__fetching)
136→                    is State.Error.NoCollection -> fragment.getString(R.string.pref__etc__summary__no_collection)
137→                    is State.Error.Exception -> fragment.getString(R.string.pref__etc__summary__error)
138→                    is State.Fetched -> preference.text
139→                }
140→            }
141→
142→            preference.onClickListener = listener@{
143→                when (val state = viewModel.flowOfState.value) {
144→                    is State.Fetching -> fragment.showSnackbar(R.string.pref__etc__snackbar__fetching)
145→                    is State.Error.NoCollection -> fragment.showSnackbar(R.string.pref__etc__snackbar__no_collection)
146→                    is State.Error.Exception -> fragment.showSnackbar(
147→                        text = fragment.requireContext().getUserFriendlyErrorText(state.exception)
148→                    )
149→                    is State.Fetched -> return@listener ShouldShowDialog.Yes
150→                }
151→                return@listener ShouldShowDialog.No
152→            }
153→        }
154→
155→        // We don't have any useful data in case the state isn't State.Fetched, but
156→        // we still need to trigger the summary providers. Setting dummy values is one simple way.
157→        fragment.lifecycleScope.launch {
158→            viewModel.flowOfState.collect { state ->
159→                val limits = if (state is State.Fetched) state.backupLimits else BackupLimits.getDefaultInstance()
160→                minutesBetweenAutomaticBackupsPreference.setValue(limits.minimumIntervalMins)
161→                dailyBackupsToKeepPreference.setValue(limits.daily)
162→                weeklyBackupsToKeepPreference.setValue(limits.weekly)
163→                monthlyBackupsToKeepPreference.setValue(limits.monthly)
164→            }
165→        }
166→    }
167→
168→    override fun onResume(owner: LifecycleOwner) { refresh() }
169→
170→    fun refresh() {
171→        viewModel.launchFetchingOfBackupLimits()
172→    }
173→
174→    fun observeLifecycle() {
175→        fragment.lifecycle.addObserver(this)
176→    }
177→
178→    // T is reified in order to make the cast checked
179→    private inline fun <reified T> Preference.launchWhenChanged(crossinline block: suspend (T) -> Unit) {
180→        setOnPreferenceChangeListener { _, newValue ->
181→            this@BackupLimitsPresenter.fragment.launchCatchingTask { block(newValue as T) }
182→            true
183→        }
184→    }
185→}

 55→class Preferences :
 56→    AnkiActivity(),

 59→
 60→    fun hasLateralNavigation(): Boolean {
 61→        return findViewById<FragmentContainerView>(R.id.lateral_nav_container) != null
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun onTitleChanged(title: CharSequence?, color: Int) {
 65→        super.onTitleChanged(title, color)
 66→        findViewById<CollapsingToolbarLayout>(R.id.collapsingToolbarLayout)?.title = title
 67→        supportActionBar?.title = title
 68→    }
 69→
 70→    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 71→        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
 72→        setContentView(R.layout.preferences)
 73→        setTransparentStatusBar()
 74→
 75→        enableToolbar().setDisplayHomeAsUpEnabled(true)
 76→
 77→        // Load initial fragment if activity is being first created
 78→        if (savedInstanceState == null) {
 79→            loadInitialFragment()
 80→        }
 81→        supportFragmentManager.addOnBackStackChangedListener {
 82→            // Expand bar in new fragments if scrolled to top
 83→            val fragment = supportFragmentManager.findFragmentById(R.id.settings_container)
 84→                as? PreferenceFragmentCompat ?: return@addOnBackStackChangedListener
 85→            fragment.listView.post {
 86→                val viewHolder = fragment.listView?.findViewHolderForAdapterPosition(0)
 87→                val isAtTop = viewHolder != null && viewHolder.itemView.top >= 0
 88→                findViewById<AppBarLayout>(R.id.appbar).setExpanded(isAtTop, false)
 89→            }
 90→        }
 91→    }
 92→
 93→    /**
 94→     * Starts the first fragment for the [Preferences] activity,
 95→     * which by default is [HeaderFragment].
 96→     * The initial fragment may be overridden by putting the java class name
 97→     * of the fragment on an intent extra with the key [INITIAL_FRAGMENT_EXTRA]
 98→     */
 99→    private fun loadInitialFragment() {
100→        val fragmentClassName = intent?.getStringExtra(INITIAL_FRAGMENT_EXTRA)
101→        val initialFragment = if (fragmentClassName == null) {
102→            if (hasLateralNavigation()) GeneralSettingsFragment() else HeaderFragment()
103→        } else {
104→            try {
105→                getInstanceFromClassName<Fragment>(fragmentClassName)
106→            } catch (e: Exception) {
107→                throw RuntimeException("Failed to load $fragmentClassName", e)
108→            }
109→        }
110→        supportFragmentManager.commit {
111→            // In tablets, show the headers fragment at the lateral navigation container
112→            if (hasLateralNavigation()) {
113→                replace(R.id.lateral_nav_container, HeaderFragment())
114→                replace(R.id.settings_container, initialFragment, initialFragment::class.java.name)
115→            } else {
116→                replace(R.id.settings_container, initialFragment, initialFragment::class.java.name)
117→            }
118→        }
119→    }
120→
121→    override fun onPreferenceStartFragment(
122→        caller: PreferenceFragmentCompat,
123→        pref: Preference
124→    ): Boolean {
125→        // avoid reopening the same fragment if already active
126→        val currentFragment = supportFragmentManager.findFragmentById(R.id.settings_container)
127→            ?: return true
128→        if (pref.fragment == currentFragment::class.jvmName) return true
129→
130→        val fragment = supportFragmentManager.fragmentFactory.instantiate(
131→            classLoader,
132→            pref.fragment ?: return true
133→        )
134→        fragment.arguments = pref.extras
135→        supportFragmentManager.commit {
136→            replace(R.id.settings_container, fragment, fragment::class.jvmName)
137→            addToBackStack(null)
138→        }
139→        return true
140→    }
141→
142→    override fun onOptionsItemSelected(item: MenuItem): Boolean {
143→        if (item.itemId == android.R.id.home) {
144→            if (hasLateralNavigation()) {
145→                finish()
146→            } else {
147→                onBackPressedDispatcher.onBackPressed()
148→            }
149→        }
150→        return true
151→    }

153→    fun restartWithNewDeckPicker() {
154→        launchCatchingTask {
155→            CollectionManager.discardBackend()
156→            val deckPicker = Intent(this@Preferences, DeckPicker::class.java)
157→            deckPicker.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK)
158→            startActivity(deckPicker)
159→        }

166→    /**

168→     */
169→    fun setDevOptionsEnabled(isEnabled: Boolean) {
170→        // Update the "devOptionsEnabledByUser" pref value
171→        this.sharedPrefs().edit {
172→            putBoolean(getString(R.string.dev_options_enabled_by_user_key), isEnabled)
173→        }
174→        // Show/hide the header
175→        val headerFragment =
176→            supportFragmentManager.findFragmentByTag(HeaderFragment::class.java.name)
177→        if (headerFragment is HeaderFragment) {
178→            headerFragment.setDevOptionsVisibility(isEnabled)
179→        }
180→    }
181→
182→    override fun onSearchResultClicked(result: SearchPreferenceResult) {
183→        val fragment = getFragmentFromXmlRes(result.resourceFile) ?: return
184→
185→        supportFragmentManager.popBackStack() // clear the search fragment from the backstack
186→        supportFragmentManager.commit {
187→            replace(R.id.settings_container, fragment, fragment.javaClass.name)
188→            addToBackStack(fragment.javaClass.name)
189→        }

191→        Timber.i("Highlighting key '%s' on %s", result.key, fragment)
192→        result.highlight(fragment as PreferenceFragmentCompat)
193→    }

 16→package com.ichi2.anki.preferences
 17→
 18→import android.content.Context
 19→import android.content.Intent
 20→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 21→import android.os.Bundle
 22→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 23→import androidx.annotation.XmlRes
 24→import androidx.core.os.bundleOf
 25→import androidx.preference.Preference
 26→import androidx.preference.PreferenceCategory
 27→import androidx.preference.PreferenceFragmentCompat
 28→import androidx.preference.PreferenceManager
 29→import androidx.preference.PreferenceManager.OnPreferenceTreeClickListener
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.analytics.UsageAnalytics
 31→import com.ichi2.preferences.DialogFragmentProvider
 32→import timber.log.Timber
 33→import java.lang.NumberFormatException
 34→import kotlin.reflect.KClass
 35→import kotlin.reflect.jvm.jvmName
 36→
 37→abstract class SettingsFragment :
 38→    PreferenceFragmentCompat(),
 39→    OnPreferenceTreeClickListener,
 40→    SharedPreferences.OnSharedPreferenceChangeListener {
 41→    /** @return The XML file which defines the preferences displayed by this PreferenceFragment
 42→     */
 43→    @get:XmlRes
 44→    abstract val preferenceResource: Int
 45→
 46→    /**
 47→     * Refreshes all values on the screen
 48→     * Call if a large number of values are changed from one preference.
 49→     */
 50→    protected fun refreshScreen() {
 51→        preferenceScreen.removeAll()
 52→        addPreferencesFromResource(preferenceResource)
 53→        initSubscreen()
 54→    }
 55→
 56→    abstract fun initSubscreen()
 57→
 58→    override fun onPreferenceTreeClick(preference: Preference): Boolean {
 59→        UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsEvent(
 60→            category = UsageAnalytics.Category.SETTING,
 61→            action = UsageAnalytics.Actions.TAPPED_SETTING,
 62→            label = preference.key
 63→        )
 64→        return super.onPreferenceTreeClick(preference)
 65→    }
 66→
 67→    override fun onSharedPreferenceChanged(sharedPreferences: SharedPreferences, key: String?) {
 68→        if (key !in UsageAnalytics.preferencesWhoseChangesShouldBeReported) {
 69→            return
 70→        }
 71→        if (key != null) {
 72→            val valueToReport = getPreferenceReportableValue(sharedPreferences.get(key))
 73→            UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsEvent(
 74→                category = UsageAnalytics.Category.SETTING,
 75→                action = UsageAnalytics.Actions.CHANGED_SETTING,
 76→                value = valueToReport,
 77→                label = key
 78→            )
 79→        }
 80→    }
 81→
 82→    override fun onCreatePreferences(savedInstanceState: Bundle?, rootKey: String?) {
 83→        UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsScreenView(analyticsScreenNameConstant)
 84→        addPreferencesFromResource(preferenceResource)
 85→        initSubscreen()
 86→    }
 87→
 88→    protected abstract val analyticsScreenNameConstant: String
 89→
 90→    @Suppress("deprecation") // setTargetFragment #9452
 91→    // androidx.preference.PreferenceDialogFragmentCompat uses the deprecated method
 92→    // `getTargetFragment()`, which throws if `setTargetFragment()` isn't used before.
 93→    // While this isn't fixed on upstream, suppress the deprecation warning
 94→    override fun onDisplayPreferenceDialog(preference: Preference) {
 95→        val dialogFragment = (preference as? DialogFragmentProvider)?.makeDialogFragment()
 96→            ?: return super.onDisplayPreferenceDialog(preference)
 97→        Timber.d("displaying custom preference: ${dialogFragment::class.simpleName}")
 98→        dialogFragment.arguments = bundleOf("key" to preference.key)
 99→        dialogFragment.setTargetFragment(this, 0)
100→        dialogFragment.show(parentFragmentManager, "androidx.preference.PreferenceFragment.DIALOG")
101→    }
102→
103→    override fun onStart() {
104→        super.onStart()
105→        requireActivity().title = preferenceScreen.title
106→        PreferenceManager.getDefaultSharedPreferences(requireContext())
107→            .registerOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener(this)
108→    }
109→
110→    override fun onStop() {
111→        super.onStop()
112→        PreferenceManager.getDefaultSharedPreferences(requireContext())
113→            .unregisterOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener(this)
114→    }
115→
116→    protected fun allPreferences(): List<Preference> {
117→        val allPreferences = mutableListOf<Preference>()
118→        for (i in 0 until preferenceScreen.preferenceCount) {
119→            val pref = preferenceScreen.getPreference(i)
120→            if (pref is PreferenceCategory) {
121→                for (j in 0 until pref.preferenceCount) {
122→                    allPreferences.add(pref.getPreference(j))
123→                }
124→            } else {
125→                allPreferences.add(pref)
126→            }
127→        }
128→        return allPreferences
129→    }
130→
131→    companion object {
132→        @JvmStatic // Using protected members which are not @JvmStatic in the superclass companion is unsupported yet
133→        protected fun getSubscreenIntent(context: Context, fragmentClass: KClass<out SettingsFragment>): Intent {
134→            return Intent(context, Preferences::class.java)
135→                .putExtra(Preferences.INITIAL_FRAGMENT_EXTRA, fragmentClass.jvmName)
136→        }
137→
138→        /**
139→         * Converts a preference value to a numeric number that
140→         * can be reported to analytics, since analytics events only accept
141→         * [Int] as value ([UsageAnalytics.sendAnalyticsEvent]),
142→         * or null if it can't be converted.
143→         *
144→         * Boolean preferences will return 1 if true and 0 if false
145→         *
146→         * String preferences whose values are stored in a numeric format,
147→         * e.g. fullscreen mode whose values are "0", "1" and "2",
148→         * can have their values reported as well.
149→         * */
150→        @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.PRIVATE)
151→        fun getPreferenceReportableValue(value: Any?): Int? {
152→            return when (value) {
153→                is Int -> value
154→                is String -> try { value.toInt() } catch (e: NumberFormatException) { null }
155→                is Boolean -> if (value) 1 else 0
156→                is Float -> value.toInt()
157→                is Long -> value.toInt()
158→                else -> null
159→            }
160→        }
161→    }
162→}
